2604.25765.title: Measuring the Sensitivity of Classification Models with the Error Sensitivity Profile
2604.25765.paper_summary: The quality of training data is critical to the performance of machine learning models. In this paper, the Error Sensitivity Profile (ESP) is proposed. It quantifies the sensitivity of model performance to errors in a single feature or in multiple features. By leveraging ESP, data-cleaning efforts can be prioritized based on error types and features most likely to affect model performance. To support the computation of this metric, an integrated suite of tools, called \dirty, is created. We conduct an extensive experimental study on two widely used datasets using 14 classification models, revealing that performance degradation is not always predictable from simple correlations with the target variable.
2604.25765.paper_summary_zh: 訓練數據的質量對機器學習模型的性能至關重要。<lb/>在本文中，提出了錯誤敏感度概況（ESP）。<lb/>它量化了模型性能對單一特徵或多個特徵中的錯誤的敏感性。<lb/>通過利用ESP，可以根據最有可能影響模型性能的錯誤類型和特徵來優先考慮數據清理工作。<lb/>為了支持這個指標的計算，創建了一套名為\dirty的集成工具。<lb/>我們在兩個廣泛使用的數據集上使用14個分類模型進行了廣泛的實驗研究，揭示了性能下降並不總是可以從與目標變量的簡單相關性中預測。
2604.25765.author: Andrea Maurino et.al.
